title: In-Flight Run Marker
description: Marker file written by an orchestration script before transient-zone cleanup and cleared in a try/finally on exit. Its presence causes a concurrent orchestration invocation to fail fast; its staleness (dead PID or stale timestamp) causes the next invocation to auto-recover.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version of this marker file.

requestId.description: Run-request GUID. Matches the requestId in the corresponding run-request.json.

invokeScript.description: Basename of the orchestration script that created this marker (e.g., invoke-input-dispatch.ps1).

pid.description: Process ID of the pwsh host running the orchestration script.

hostname.description: Host machine name — used together with pid to disambiguate PID reuse across machines sharing a network-mounted project.

startedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the marker was written. Consumers compute staleness as (now - startedAt) vs 2× orchestration timeout.

toolVersion.description: Optional semver of the harness tooling that wrote the marker. Useful for post-mortems when a stale marker is recovered.
